def: Pertaining to, or characterized by, implements or their use;mechanical.
To interpose; to insert or place between. [R.] Cudworth.
The state of being excessively or abnormally full, so as tocause overflow, distention, or congestion; excess of fullness;surfeit.
A variety of Cratægus, or hawthorn (C. Crus-galli), havinglong, straight thorns; -- called also Cockspur thorn.
A doer or actor; particularly, an evil doer; a scoundrel.[Obs.]Lo! faitour, there thy meed unto thee take. Spenser.
Producing ducks; -- applied to Anatifæ, under the absurd notionof their turning into ducks or geese. See Barnacle.
Of the nature of agate, or containing agate.
